subject: Project Management
title: Gothic Cathedrals
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Gothic Cathedrals.

hook.text: Notre-Dame took 182 years to build. No single person saw it start and finish.

buildup.text: Master builders passed plans to apprentices who became masters and trained their own.

discovery.text: Flying buttresses were invented mid-project to solve a structural crisis.

twist.text: Most cathedrals had no architect — just master masons and evolving plans.

climax.text: Multi-generational projects required something PMs still struggle with: knowledge transfer.

punchline.text: The longest handoff in history took centuries.

quiz.question: How long did Notre-Dame de Paris take to build?
